The Introductory Hymn First Strophe In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
21 And he answered and said unto them, My mother and my breihren are those who hear the word of God, and do it.

In gospel-sharing homes we pray for guidance for ourselves, and we pray for the physical and spiritual well-being of others. We pray for the people the missionaries are teaching, for our acquaintances, and for those not of our faith. In the gospel-sharing homes of Alma's time, the people would "join in fasting and mighty prayer in behalf of the welfare of the souls of those who knew not God" (Alma 6:6). (M. Russell Ballard, in Conference Report, Apr. 2006, 88; or Ensign, May 2006, 85)

Thank you also for praying for the missionaries. Everyone prays for the missionaries. May it ever be so. In that same spirit, we should also pray for those who are (or who need to be) meeting the missionaries. In Zarahemla, members were commanded to “join in fasting and mighty prayer” for those who had not yet joined the Church of God. We can do the same. (Jeffrey R. Holland, in Conference Report, Apr. 2001, 16; or Ensign, May 2001, 15)

are the seedbed for revelation. The classic illustration of this principle for our day came by way of instruction to three of the early missionaries of the Church. "My servant, Orson Hyde,' the revelation begins, "was called by his ordination to proclaim the everlasting gospel, by the Spirit of the living God, from people to people, and from land to land, in the congregations of the wicked, in their synagogues, reasoning with and expounding all scriptures unto them. And, behold, and lo, this is…
